Nuclear facility status
IAEA · CENTCOM · verified reports
☢️

Nuclear facility status — auto-updated hourly from Wikipedia and IAEA statements. ⚠ All figures approximate and unconfirmed. Verify directly with iaea.org

Natanz Enrichment Facility
Status: see latest reports · ⚠ approx
Auto-updated from Wikipedia · ⚠ approximate · unconfirmed
60%-Enriched Uranium Stock
Location Unknown
Auto-updated from Wikipedia · ⚠ approximate · unconfirmed
Fordow Enrichment Plant
Status Unclear
Underground near Qom. Built inside mountain to survive conventional strikes. Strike status not officially confirmed. · ⚠ Last verified 21 Mar 2026 23:53 UTC
Arak Heavy Water Reactor
Reportedly Intact
No confirmed strikes reported. Plutonium production — distinct risk profile from enrichment sites. · ⚠ Last verified 21 Mar 2026 23:53 UTC
Iranian Nuclear Scientists
9+ Killed
Israel reportedly killed 9+ named scientists. Fereydoon Abbasi and Mohammad Mehdi Tehranchi confirmed. · ⚠ Last verified 21 Mar 2026 23:53 UTC
IAEA Verification
Suspended
Inspectors cannot verify facility status or uranium stockpiles during active conflict. · ⚠ Last verified 21 Mar 2026 23:53 UTC

⚠ Data Sources & Accuracy
Sources & methodology — All figures sourced from publicly available, named organisations. Casualty figures shown from multiple independent sources simultaneously to reflect genuine uncertainty — we do not endorse any single figure as definitive. Updated manually; figures reflect best available information as of the displayed timestamp.
Primary sources: Al Jazeera live tracker · Hengaw Organisation for Human Rights · HRANA · CENTCOM (US Central Command) · Wikipedia 2026 Iran War article · NPR Iran War Cost Tracker · CNBC Energy · Bloomberg · Fortune · Newsweek · TIME · IEA · IAEA statements · Iran Health Ministry (via Reuters/AP)
⚡ Data auto-updated 27 Apr 2026 22:08 UTC · Scraped from Wikipedia, Al Jazeera, Reuters, BBC & oilpriceapi.com · Figures are approximate and unconfirmed · Always verify with primary sources
ADVERTISEMENT — YOUR AD HERE
WARWATCH WarIntel.info · Worldwide Realtime Statistics
Disclaimer Privacy Policy Terms of Use Sources
⚠ Disclaimer & Accuracy Notice

WarIntel is an independent data aggregation dashboard. All information is sourced from publicly available third-party sources including Wikipedia, Al Jazeera, Reuters, BBC, Hengaw, HRANA, CENTCOM, IAEA, IEA, Bloomberg, CNBC, and NPR. Data is scraped automatically and may be delayed, incomplete, or inaccurate.

WarIntel makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of any data displayed. All figures are approximate and unconfirmed unless explicitly stated otherwise. Casualty figures are particularly contested and subject to significant revision. The display of multiple conflicting figures is intentional and reflects genuine uncertainty.

Nothing on this site constitutes professional, financial, legal, medical, military, or investment advice. WarIntel expressly disclaims all liability for any loss or harm arising from reliance on information presented here. Always verify with primary sources.

WarIntel is not affiliated with any government, military, intelligence agency, political party, news organisation, or advocacy group.

🔒 Privacy Policy

Last updated: March 2026. This Privacy Policy applies to the WarIntel website operated from the Republic of South Africa.

Data we collect: WarIntel does not directly collect or store personal data. Panel preferences and text size settings are stored locally in your browser using localStorage and never transmitted to any server.

Third-party advertising: This site may display advertisements served by third-party networks including Google AdSense and/or Ezoic. These networks may use cookies to serve personalised advertisements. You can opt out at adssettings.google.com.

Your rights: Under South Africa's Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA) and the EU GDPR, you have rights over your personal data. Since we do not collect personal data directly, no such data exists on our systems. For advertising-related data, contact the respective ad network.

📋 Terms of Use

Permitted use: You may access and view content for personal, non-commercial informational purposes. You may share links to this site freely.

Prohibited use: You may not reproduce, scrape, redistribute, or commercially exploit any content, data, design, or code from this site without prior written permission.

Intellectual property: The design, layout, code, and original presentation are the intellectual property of WarIntel. Underlying data belongs to the respective third-party sources.

Limitation of liability: To the fullest extent permitted by law, WarIntel and its operators shall not be liable for any damages arising from your use of this site.

Governing law: These terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa.

📡 Sources & Methodology

Automated data sources: Wikipedia (2026 Iran–United States war article) · Al Jazeera RSS · Reuters RSS · BBC RSS · oilpriceapi.com · IAEA statements.

Methodology: Data is scraped automatically every hour using a GitHub Actions workflow. Figures are injected directly from source text and are not manually verified before publication. All figures should be treated as approximate and unconfirmed.

Update frequency: Oil prices update every hour. News headlines update every hour. Casualty and strike figures update when Wikipedia is updated, which varies from minutes to hours. The timestamp shown reflects when the last automated update ran.

Corrections: Contact us via X @warintellive. We take accuracy seriously and will update promptly upon verification.

⚠ Data Sources & Accuracy
Casualty figures — Iran Health Ministry (via Wikipedia), Hengaw (hengaw.net), HRANA (en-hrana.org). Approximate, unconfirmed, subject to revision.
Strike & missile data — Wikipedia "2026 Iran–United States war". Cumulative estimates, may lag real-time events.
Oil & energy prices — oilpriceapi.com. Spot estimates, not for trading purposes.
Nuclear status — IAEA statements and CENTCOM press releases via Wikipedia. Editorial summaries only.
News headlines — Al Jazeera, Reuters, AP, BBC, The Guardian, France 24 RSS feeds.
War cost — Formula-based estimate from Pentagon and CSIS briefing data. Not an official figure.
Update frequency — All data refreshes every 6 hours. Nothing here constitutes professional, financial, legal, or military advice.
© 2026 WarIntel · All rights reserved · Operated from the Republic of South Africa Corrections: @warintellive
